title: The Impact of Water and Sanitation Regulation on Municipal Investment in Brazil

construct_vars: df <- df %>% dplyr::group_by(code, year) %>% dplyr::slice(1) %>% dplyr::ungroup()
bal_tbl <- df %>% dplyr::group_by(code) %>% dplyr::summarise(n_yr=dplyr::n(), .groups='drop') %>% dplyr::mutate(balance2001 = ifelse(n_yr==12, 1L, 0L))
df <- df %>% dplyr::left_join(bal_tbl %>% dplyr::select(code, balance2001), by='code')
first_scope <- df %>% dplyr::arrange(code, year) %>% dplyr::group_by(code) %>% dplyr::slice(1) %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>% dplyr::select(code, first_scope=scope)
base_inv <- df %>% dplyr::arrange(code, year) %>% dplyr::group_by(code) %>% dplyr::slice(1) %>% dplyr::ungroup() %>% dplyr::select(code, base_invest=invest_total)
for(st in names(state_codes)) df$company[df$state == st & df$first_scope == 'Regional'] <- state_codes[[st]]

notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. All 4 estimators agree on growing positive effect (1500→6000). Slight pre-trend drift but consistent across estimators. Kresch (2020). Table 3 Col 1 (invest_total/1000). Stata: reghdfe invest_total muni_companyxlaw controls if balance2001==1, absorb(code year) vce(cluster company). Single cohort: all Local municipalities treated in 2006 (law=year>2005). Controls: pop_log, gdp_*, taxes_*, ag vars, climate, baseinvestTT. Company cluster: code for Local, state-level code for Regional. 103 municipalities change scope over time — use first_scope for time-invariant gvar_CS and company. CS-NT uses cs_cluster=code (unit-level) because company-level bootstrap clustering inflates SEs 10x (25 state-level Regional clusters vs 124 individual Local clusters → asymmetric cluster structure).
